title: trema timorense var. pallidum
intro: Trema timorense var. pallidum, also known as the Timor trema or pallid trema, is a plant species within the family Cannabaceae. It is a climbing shrub native to the tropical forests of Timor and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its health benefits in reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing. The herbal preparations of Trema timorense var. pallidum include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, and salves for medicinal purposes.
